sections.paragraph:
εἰκάζω 1 aor. inf. εἰκάσαι (Wsd 19:18), opt. εἰκάσαιμι, pass. subj. εἰκασθῇ (Jer 26:23) to form a mental image of someth., suppose, imagine (so Aeschyl., Hdt. et al.; PBerlSchubart 4, 32 [III a.d., lit.]; Wsd; En 21:7; EpArist 105; Philo, Fuga 179; Jos., Ant. 17, 136, Vi. 148) Dg 2:3 cj. Lachmann; 7:2. Cp. ‘appraise, value (at)’ in pap fr. III b.c., also PMichael 18 Acol. 1, 6 (III a.d.).—DELG s.v. ἔοικα. Sv.

aor. aor. = aorist

inf. inf. = infinitive

opt. opt. = optative

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

subj. subj. = subjunctive; subject

someth. someth. = something

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

En En = I Enoch—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

  1. cj. = conjecture(d by)

Lachmann Lachmann = KLachmann. This abbreviation used when no other editors of the Gk. NT are mentioned.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

  1. fr. = from

PMichael PMichael = Papyri Michaelidae, a collection by GAMichaïlidis of Cairo—List 4

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 281
